missing_elements: User Personas / Actors: Only 'customer' is implied; no explicit roles (e.g., cluster admin configuring ExternalSecretsConfig vs application team authoring SecretStore)
Acceptance Criteria & Edge Cases: No Given/When/Then scenarios, no negative cases (invalid ConfigMap, missing CA, proxy-only vs custom CA coexistence), no upgrade/migration behavior

Scope Boundaries: Unclear whether changes are limited to downstream operator (ExternalSecretsConfig operand wiring) vs upstream operand CRDs (SecretStore/ClusterSecretStore CA fields); cert-manager Operator listed as affected but integration scope not defined
Dependencies: No explicit dependency on cluster proxy configuration, OCP trusted CA bundle injection mechanics, or minimum OpenShift/ESO versions
Operational semantics: No uninstall/reconcile behavior when CA reference is removed or ConfigMap is deleted

quality_issues.quote: Adding an option to reference a ConfigMap containing the trusted CA certificates
quality_issues.suggestion: Define the API field shape (name/namespace/key or well-known path), whether reference is cluster- or namespace-scoped, and which workloads receive the mount (controller only, webhook only, or both).

quality_issues.quote: Extending the API to include a CA reference for server verification
quality_issues.suggestion: Clarify whether the CA reference belongs on operator CRDs (ExternalSecretsConfig/ExternalSecretsManager), upstream SecretStore/ClusterSecretStore, or both; specify if this affects outbound TLS from the operand controller only or also provider connections initiated by SecretStore reconciliation.

quality_issues.quote: Resolve the x509: certificate signed by unknown authority error
quality_issues.suggestion: Add measurable acceptance criteria, e.g., 'Given a SecretStore targeting an internal vault signed by enterprise CA in ConfigMap X, When ExternalSecret sync runs, Then sync succeeds without x509 unknown authority errors and ESC reports Ready.'

quality_issues.quote: The operator only injects the OpenShift trusted CA bundle into the well-known path (/etc/pki/tls/certs) if a cluster-wide proxy is configured
quality_issues.suggestion: State expected behavior when both cluster proxy CA injection and custom enterprise CA ConfigMap are configured: merge order, path layout, and whether custom CA supplements or replaces proxy-injected bundle.

quality_issues.quote: Extending the API... Adding an option to reference a ConfigMap... controller and webhook deployments... SecretStore and ClusterSecretStore
quality_issues.suggestion: This spans operator API, operand deployment reconciliation, and upstream CRD surface. Consider splitting into operator CA injection (ExternalSecretsConfig) vs provider CA reference (SecretStore) if they are independently deliverable.

quality_issues.quote: Affected Packages: External Secrets Operator... CRDs: SecretStore and ClusterSecretStore... OCP Trusted CA Bundle Injection
quality_issues.suggestion: Reconcile operator-owned vs operand-owned API boundaries: the OpenShift operator deploys upstream ESO but does not own SecretStore/ClusterSecretStore schema evolution on all branches. Align the spec with which layer this change targets on release-1.1.

non_blockers: No explicit user personas or RBAC implications for who may reference enterprise CA ConfigMaps
Missing acceptance criteria and edge cases for ConfigMap lifecycle (update, delete, invalid PEM)
Unclear interaction between proxy-triggered OCP trusted CA injection and custom enterprise CA
cert-manager Operator mentioned as affected component without defined integration requirement
No upgrade semantics for clusters already using unsupported manual volume workarounds
